structured_tensions.tension_text: Lindy-effect conflicts with creative-destruction when the replacement dynamic of new ideas cuts against the evidentiary value of longevity.
structured_tensions.source_quote: lindy-effect conflicts with creative-destruction (creative destruction) when creative Destruction emphasizes the constant replacement of old ideas with new ones, contrasting with the Lindy Effect's focus on longevity.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Lindy-effect conflicts with black-swan-events when rare discontinuities can abruptly end a long-lived baseline.
structured_tensions.source_quote: lindy-effect conflicts with black-swan-events (black swan events) when black Swan Events highlight unpredictable, rare occurrences that can abruptly end the longevity of ideas, opposing the Lindy Effect's predictability.

curation_notes.summary: Preserved only current-vocabulary first-order seams that were explicit or cleanly warranted by the raw source.
donor_drops: Did not keep donor-shaped or out-of-vocabulary relation surfaces; omission beat weak forcing.
open_questions: Higher-order candidates remain observational only and belong in later selective review rather than in this closure batch.

allies: Wave 4 candidacy remains observational only for this model.
antagonists: Any broader antagonistic surface not preserved here was omitted because the current first-order vocabulary did not justify forcing it.
structured_tensions: Only explicit or cleanly source-supported tensions that survive current-vocabulary discipline were preserved.
